strands.title: hamutay Phase 1 EXECUTION — CYCLE 50 PRE-EXECUTION VALIDATION & SMOKE TEST INITIATED
strands.content: CYCLE 50 INTEGRATION: Phase 1 execution pathway PROCEEDING (Cycle 49 priority decision: hamutay Phase 1 proceeds immediately; replay module queued for Cycle 50-51 design). CYCLE 49 CHECKPOINT confirmed: log_reader.py patched (turn 147), Phase 4a DEFERRED, Phase 1 UNBLOCKED. CYCLE 50 ACTION (turn 148-150): PRE-EXECUTION VALIDATION PHASE INITIATED. (1) Turn 148: log_reader.py patch CONFIRMED DEPLOYED (file updated successfully). (2) Turn 149-150: Smoke test protocol INITIATED — uv sync executed to refresh dependencies before Haiku API token expenditure. RATIONALE: Catch import errors, version conflicts, missing dependencies BEFORE attempting full run_projection.py execution. PHILOSOPHY: Validate infrastructure (Python imports, module paths, dependency resolution) using zero-cost operations (local filesystem checks, uv sync) before incurring API costs. CYCLE 50 STATUS: smoke test results PENDING. Assuming uv sync succeeds, next action = test run_projection.py CLI parsing + log_reader on sample session (first 5 turns) to verify parsing correctness without full API execution. EXPECTED OUTCOME (Cycle 50): (1) uv sync succeeds (no unresolved dependencies), (2) run_projection.py --help works + argparse recognized, (3) log_reader.read_session_jsonl() parses first 5 turns from baseline_run1 session without error, (4) THEN PROCEED to full Phase 1 execution (Cycle 50-51 main phase, will incur API costs).

key_claims.text: Cycle 50 Phase 1 execution decision LOCKED IN: hamutay proceeds immediately; replay module parallel work (Cycle 50-51).
log_reader.py patch (turn 148) CONFIRMED DEPLOYED: file updated successfully at /home/tony/projects/hamutay/src/hamutay/log_reader.py.
Cycle 50 smoke test protocol: uv sync (dependency validation), then CLI parsing + sample parsing before full execution.
Zero-cost pre-execution validation strategy: catch import/version errors locally before incurring Haiku API costs.
Next action after smoke test success: test run_projection.py CLI + log_reader sample parse (first 5 turns) without API calls.

strands.title: PICHAY REPLAY MODULE — CYCLE 50 CONTEXT (ENQUEUED, NOT YET DESIGNED)
strands.content: CYCLE 49-50 CONTEXT: Pichay replay module (turn 145 specification) enqueued as parallel work. NOT BLOCKING Phase 1 execution (Cycle 49 decision: Phase 1 proceeds, replay design follows Cycle 50-51). CYCLE 50 STATUS: Replay module DEFERRED; Phase 1 smoke test takes priority. SPECIFICATION REMAINS (turn 145): Build src/pichay/replay.py parser + reconstruction + page fault detection + stats aggregation. Prerequisites: read pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py. CYCLE 50-51 PLAN (if Phase 1 smoke test succeeds): (1) Cycle 50, Stage 1 (code review): inspect pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py to understand proxy JSONL format, pager compaction algorithm, eval stats patterns. (2) Cycle 50-51, Stage 2 (design): develop replay module architecture (parser, reconstruction logic, page fault detector, CLI). (3) Cycle 51, Stage 3 (implementation): code replay.py, test on small proxy logs, validate stats. CARRY-FORWARD: Replay module prerequisites (proxy.py JSONL format, page fault detection definition, pager.py integration) remain OPEN questions — will be resolved during Cycle 50 code review stage.

key_claims.text: Pichay replay module enqueued (turn 145) as PARALLEL work, not blocking Phase 1.
Replay module design/implementation scheduled Cycle 50-51 (contingent on Phase 1 smoke test success).
Cycle 50 priority: Phase 1 smoke test + pre-execution validation takes precedence over replay module code review.
Replay module prerequisites (proxy.py format, page fault definition) remain OPEN; will resolve in Cycle 50-51 code review.

strands.title: CYCLE 50 OPEN QUESTIONS — REFINED & PRIORITIZED FOR IMMEDIATE RESOLUTION
strands.content: CYCLE 50 OPEN QUESTIONS (refined from Cycle 49, now prioritized by smoke test phase): IMMEDIATE (CYCLE 50, SMOKE TEST STAGE): (1) uv sync result: Did dependency sync complete without errors? (Python version conflict? Missing packages?) (2) run_projection.py CLI: Does argparse correctly recognize baseline_run1 session path, output_tensors dir, --max_turns flag? (3) log_reader sample parse: Does read_session_jsonl() successfully parse first 5 turns from Claude Code format without errors? (4) session JSONL accessibility: Is baseline_run1 session file readable at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl? (5) Haiku API availability: Is ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var set + Haiku model accessible (not rate-limited or auth error)? SECONDARY (CYCLE 50-51, FULL EXECUTION STAGE): (6) Haiku token count accuracy: Does tensor.token_estimate() match actual Haiku token counts? (determines trajectory duration). (7) Tensor sanity thresholds: Cycle 50 results will establish concrete SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL boundaries. (8) Strand coherence & stability: Do strands maintain semantic consistency across cycles? Or do they drift/merge unexpectedly? TERTIARY (CYCLE 51+, POST-PHASE-1): (9) Phase 1+ Riemann metrics: Can d_strand and d_claim be reliably computed from tensor trajectory? (10) Replay module prerequisites: proxy.py JSONL schema, page fault detection algorithm, pager.py integration pattern. (DEFERRED to Cycle 50-51 code review). CARRY-FORWARD (UNRESOLVED): Phase 4a sufficiency for all baseline_run* sessions; projector initialization correctness on first tensor; Phase 1 output destination (where to store final trajectory JSON?).

key_claims.text: Cycle 50 immediate questions focus on smoke test validation: uv sync, CLI parsing, sample log_reader parse, session accessibility.
Secondary questions (Cycle 50-51): Haiku token accuracy, tensor sanity thresholds, strand coherence determined by full execution results.
Tertiary questions (Cycle 51+): Phase 1+ Riemann metrics, replay module prerequisites (deferred to Cycle 50-51 code review).
Unresolved carries forward: Phase 4a sufficiency, projector initialization, Phase 1 output storage location.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed execution timeline projection (Cycle 49 instructions_for_next contained 150+ lines of step-by-step Phase 1 procedures). NOT PRESERVED in full verbatim in Cycle 50 tensor.
declared_losses.why: instructions_for_next is meta-guidance for NEXT cycle, not part of semantic state continuity. Cycle 50 tensor captures DECISION (Phase 1 proceeds) and ACTION (smoke test initiated), not the procedural roadmap. Procedural details are IMPLEMENTATION-LEVEL (not epistemically significant for next inference cycle).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Granular ERROR RECOVERY procedures (Cycle 49 instructions_for_next included detailed fallback handling for import errors, parse errors, API errors, CLI args errors). These are OPERATIONAL not EPISTEMICALLY SIGNIFICANT.
declared_losses.why: Error recovery procedures are conditional/reactive (IF error X THEN do Y). They do not contribute to ongoing semantic integration unless error actually occurs. Next cycle can re-derive recovery steps from context if needed. Preserving error procedures as strands would bloat tensor without adding epistemic content.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Partial detailed Cycle 49 DECISION TREE for Phase 1+ (Cycle 49 instructions contained IF SANE / IF PATHOLOGICAL / IF PARTIAL SUCCESS branches for Cycle 50). This is not lost; it is INSTANTIATED in Cycle 50 open_questions + next instructions.
declared_losses.why: Decision tree is not semantic state; it is PROCEDURAL PLAN. Cycle 50 tensor captures the DECISION LOCK (Phase 1 proceeds) and the immediate ACTION (smoke test). Decision tree branches will be invoked in Cycle 50-51 based on execution results. Preserving it verbatim would duplicate information already implicit in Phase 1 status + open_questions.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed REPLAY MODULE DESIGN STAGES (Cycle 49 instructions_for_next outlined 3-stage plan: code review, design, implementation). NOT PRESERVED in Cycle 50 strand.
declared_losses.why: Replay module is PARALLEL, not immediate. Cycle 50 primary focus is Phase 1 smoke test. Replay module staging will be executed Cycle 50-51 when code review can begin. Preserving 3-stage plan in Cycle 50 tensor would distract from immediate Phase 1 validation priority. Plan is RECOVERABLE from Cycle 49 tensor if needed.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 49 SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL THRESHOLDS (>70% strand stability, 50-150 token/cycle, 2-5 losses/cycle, etc.). These are PROVISIONAL THRESHOLDS established for Cycle 50 trajectory evaluation.
declared_losses.why: Thresholds are PROVISIONAL; not part of current semantic state. Cycle 50 full execution will provide ACTUAL data. Provisional thresholds will be VALIDATED or REVISED in Cycle 50-51 analysis phase. Carrying provisional thresholds forward as committed facts risks falsity if actual trajectory contradicts them.

open_questions: Did uv sync complete successfully? Any dependency version conflicts or missing packages?
Does run_projection.py --help work correctly? Are CLI argparse args (session_jsonl_path, output_dir, --max_turns) recognized?
Can log_reader.read_session_jsonl() successfully parse the first 5 turns from baseline_run1 session without errors?
Is the baseline_run1 session file accessible at the expected path? (/home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl)
Is ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable set and valid? Is Haiku model accessible without rate-limit or auth errors?
What is the actual turn count in baseline_run1 session? Does it exceed reasonable limits for Cycle 50 execution (~20 min, ~15-20 turns)?
After full Phase 1 execution: is trajectory SANE (stable strands, bounded tokens, 2-5 losses/cycle) or PATHOLOGICAL (chaotic, unbounded, or zero compression)?
Do strands maintain semantic coherence across cycles? Or do they drift, merge, or fragment unexpectedly?
Does tensor.token_estimate() match actual Haiku token consumption? Or is it a loose heuristic that breaks phase duration estimates?
Is Phase 4a patch (log_reader.py) sufficient for ALL baseline_run* sessions, or might other sessions have different JSONL formats?
Where should final Phase 1 tensor trajectory be stored? (./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_*.json is assumed; confirm this is intended output location.)
Replay module design: what is the exact proxy.py JSONL schema? Does it log full message snapshots at every turn or only at API boundaries?
Replay module: how is 'page fault' formally defined? Is it re-request of evicted content? How is 'reference' detected (string match, semantic, token ID)?
Should run_projection.py include --max_turns flag for fast feedback iteration, or always process full session?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 50 IMMEDIATE: SMOKE TEST PHASE (NEXT 10-15 MINUTES)",
  "",
  "GOAL: Validate infrastructure + pre-execution checks before full Phase 1 API execution.",
  "",
  "STEP 1: VERIFY UV SYNC RESULT (turn 150)",
  "  → If uv sync succeeded: dependency resolution OK, proceed to STEP 2",
  "  → If uv sync FAILED with error: diagnose (Python version? missing packages?) and fix before proceeding",
  "  → Expected: 'All virtual envs are up to date' or similar success message",
  "",
  "STEP 2: TEST RUN_PROJECTION.PY CLI (this turn, ~2 min)",
  "  COMMAND: python -m hamutay.run_projection --help",
  "  EXPECTED OUTPUT: argparse help showing:",
  "    - positional arg: session_jsonl_path",
  "    - positional arg: output_dir",
  "    - optional: --max_turns (if implemented)",
  "  ACTION IF FAILED:",
  "    - Check __main__.py entry point",
  "    - Verify argparse setup in run_projection.py",
  "    - Check hamutay module __init__.py exports",
  "",
  "STEP 3: TEST LOG_READER SAMPLE PARSE (this turn, ~3-5 min)",
  "  GOAL: Verify log_reader.read_session_jsonl() handles Claude Code format WITHOUT API calls",
  "  PSEUDO-CODE:",
  "    from hamutay.log_reader import read_session_jsonl",
  "    turns = read_session_jsonl('/home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl')",
  "    print(f'Parsed {len(turns)} turns')",
  "    for i, turn in enumerate(turns[:3]):",
  "      print(f'  Turn {i}: role={turn.role}, content_len={len(turn.content)}')",
  "",
  "  EXPECTED OUTPUT:",
  "    - Parsed N turns (where N > 0, typically 20-100 for baseline_run1)",
  "    - First 3 turns display: role (user|assistant), content length",
  "  ACTION IF FAILED:",
  "    - Error message indicates log_reader issue (malformed JSON, format mismatch, missing fields)",
  "    - Add debug print to read_session_jsonl() to inspect raw entry structure",
  "    - Compare against expected format: {\"type\": \"user\"|\"assistant\", \"message\": {\"role\": \"...\", \"content\": \"...\"}",
  "",
  "STEP 4: DETERMINE EXECUTION STRATEGY (this turn, ~2 min)",
  "  IF smoke tests pass (CLI + sample parse OK):",
  "    → PROCEED to PHASE 1 FULL EXECUTION (STEP 5)",
  "",
  "  IF smoke tests FAIL (CLI or parse error):",
  "    → DIAGNOSE + FIX (estimate time: 5-15 min)",
  "    → Re-run failed test",
  "    → If fix successful, PROCEED to STEP 5",
  "    → If fix unsuccessful, ESCALATE (note issue + defer Phase 1 to Cycle 51)",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 5: PHASE 1 FULL EXECUTION (Cycle 50, MAIN PHASE, ~20-60 min depending on turn count + API latency)",
  "",
  "IF SMOKE TESTS PASS, EXECUTE:",
  "  COMMAND (RECOMMENDED WITH MAX_TURNS FOR FAST FEEDBACK):",
  "    cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./output_tensors/ \\",
  "      --max_turns=10",
  "",
  "  OR IF --max_turns NOT IMPLEMENTED:",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./output_tensors/",
  "",
  "  EXPECTED OUTPUT:",
  "    - Per-turn terminal output: 'Cycle N: M strands, K key_claims, L losses, Q open_questions'",
  "    - JSON files: ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_001.json through tensor_cycle_N.json",
  "    - Final summary (if implemented): token growth chart, strand stability, loss distribution",
  "",
  "  API RATE LIMITS:",
  "    - Haiku has ~50 requests/min limit (likely)",
  "    - If run_projection hits rate limit: add --delay 1.5 between calls (if flag supported)",
  "    - Or use --max_turns to limit execution to 15 turns for Cycle 50 feedback",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "STEP 6: TRAJECTORY INSPECTION & SANITY ASSESSMENT (Cycle 50-51, ~20-30 min)",
  "",
  "AFTER run_projection.py COMPLETES, ANALYZE RESULTS:",
  "",
  "QUICK CHECKS (RED FLAG DETECTION):",
  "  □ File generation: Were tensor_cycle_*.json files created? If not, parse error or API failure.",
  "  □ Cycle count: How many cycles? (should match turn count from log_reader)",
  "  □ Strand evolution: Read first 3 cycles + last cycle, print strand titles. Are they sensible?",
  "  □ Token growth: Extract tensor.overall_token_estimate from each cycle. Is it bounded (50-150/cycle) or runaway (>300)?",
  "  □ Losses per cycle: Count declared_losses in each cycle. Healthy = 2-5. Red flags: 0 (no compression) or >10 (too aggressive).",
  "  □ Claim T/I/F distribution: Sample 20 claims from middle cycles. Are T values mostly 0.8-0.95? Or scattered?",
  "",
  "TRAJECTORY COHERENCE (deeper inspection):",
  "  1. Open tensor_cycle_005.json (middle cycle, sample)",
  "  2. Read 'strands' array: Are strand titles related to Pichay baseline task? (e.g., 'Context Paging Design', 'Baseline Implementation')",
  "  3. Check key_claims: Are claims grounded in turn content? Or vague/hallucinatory?",
  "  4. Check declared_losses: Are loss reasons (context_pressure, authorial_choice, etc.) justified? Or arbitrary?",
  "  5. Compare strand titles across cycles (cycle 1 vs cycle 5 vs final): >70% overlap = STABLE, <50% = CHAOTIC",
  "",
  "DECISION OUTPUT (record for Cycle 51):",
  "  SANE trajectory =",
  "    ✓ File generation successful",
  "    ✓ Strands remain coherent (same titles across cycles)",
  "    ✓ Token estimates 50-150 per cycle (bounded)",
  "    ✓ 2-5 losses per cycle (appropriate compression)",
  "    ✓ Claim T values mostly 0.8-0.95 (confident, grounded)",
  "    ✓ <50% strand churn per cycle",
  "",
  "  PATHOLOGICAL trajectory =",
  "    ✗ Parse/API failures (broken JSON, empty files, API errors)",
  "    ✗ Strands chaotic (new strands every cycle, no stability)",
  "    ✗ Token estimates <20 or >300 (unbounded or collapsed)",
  "    ✗ >10 losses per cycle or 0 losses (aggressive/no-op)",
  "    ✗ Claim T values <0.5 or I>0.7 (incoherent, uncertain)",
  "    ✗ >50% strand churn per cycle",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "CYCLE 50-51 DECISION TREE (based on trajectory assessment):",
  "",
  "  IF SANE:",
  "    CYCLE 51 ACTIONS:",
  "    (1) Document Phase 1 findings: strand names + stability ratio, token growth trajectory, loss types + reasons",
  "    (2) ACTIVATE PHASE 1+ METRICS: Compute d_strand (strand distance), d_claim (claim drift) from trajectory",
  "    (3) BEGIN PHASE 2 DESIGN: minimal spec doc (2-3 pages) outlining Phase 2 objectives",
  "    (4) PARALLEL: CODE REVIEW for Pichay replay module (Cycle 50-51): read pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py",
  "",
  "  IF PATHOLOGICAL:",
  "    CYCLE 51 DEBUG PHASE:",
  "    (1) Determine root cause: is it projector logic, Haiku summaries, log_reader, token_estimate?",
  "    (2) Targeted inspection: pick 1 problematic cycle (e.g., cycle 3 where churn spiked), examine full JSON + input turn content",
  "    (3) Hypothesis: projector mixes unrelated content? Haiku is drifting? log_reader mangled input?",
  "    (4) If simple fix identified: apply + re-run with --max_turns=5",
  "    (5) If systemic issue: schedule Cycle 51-52 redesign (projector algorithm or Haiku prompt engineering)",
  "",
  "  IF PARTIAL SUCCESS (e.g., first 5 cycles sane, then fails):",
  "    (1) Analyze failure point: does failure correlate with turn count? Content type? API rate limit?",
  "    (2) If turn-count dependent: may be token_estimate error (breaks compression at high counts). Check cycle N-1 token growth.",
  "    (3) If content-type dependent: projector may struggle with specific conversation patterns. Sample turn that triggered failure.",
  "    (4) Continue analysis on successful cycles (1-5), document failure threshold for Cycle 51.",
  "",
  "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "CHECKPOINT (END CYCLE 50):",
  "  ✓ UV sync completed: dependencies resolved",
  "  ✓ Smoke tests passed: CLI + sample parse verified",
  "  ✓ Phase 1 execution completed: tensor trajectory JSON files generated",
  "  ✓ Trajectory analysis: SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL assessment with evidence",
  "  ✓ Cycle 50-51 path determined: proceed to Phase 1+ metrics OR Phase 1 debugging",
  "",
  "NEXT CYCLE (51): Execute based on Cycle 50 assessment. If SANE → Phase 1+ metrics + replay module design. If PATHOLOGICAL → debug + iterate."
]
